texts.NKJV: For the Lord Himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of an archangel, and with the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first.
texts.ESV: For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first.
texts.NLT: For the Lord himself will come down from heaven with a commanding shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trumpet call of God. First, the believers who have died will rise from their graves.
texts.LEB: For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a shout of command, with the voice of the archangel and with the trumpet of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.

texts.NIV84: After that, we who are still alive and are left will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And so we will be with the Lord forever.
texts.KJV: Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.
texts.NASB95: Then we who are alive and remain will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we shall always be with the Lord.
texts.DRB: Then we who are alive, who are left, shall be taken up together with them in the clouds to meet Christ, into the air, and so shall we be always with the Lord.
texts.NKJV: Then we who are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And thus we shall always be with the Lord.
texts.ESV: Then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord.
texts.NLT: Then, together with them, we who are still alive and remain on the earth will be caught up in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. Then we will be with the Lord forever.
texts.LEB: Then we who are alive, who remain, will be snatched away at the same time together with them in the clouds for a meeting with the Lord in the air, and thus we will be together with the Lord always.

texts.NIV84: Therefore encourage each other with these words.
texts.KJV: Wherefore comfort one another with these words.
texts.NASB95: Therefore comfort one another with these words.
texts.DRB: Wherefore, comfort ye one another with these words.
texts.NKJV: Therefore comfort one another with these words.
texts.ESV: Therefore encourage one another with these words.
texts.NLT: So encourage each other with these words.
texts.LEB: Therefore comfort one another with these words.

blocks.title: Honor Is Not Fitting for a Fool

texts.NIV84: Like snow in summer or rain in harvest, honor is not fitting for a fool.
texts.KJV: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour is not seemly for a fool.
texts.NASB95:
Like snow in summer and like rain in harvest,
So honor is not fitting for a fool.
texts.DRB: As snow in summer, and rain in harvest, so glory is not seemly for a fool.
texts.NKJV:
As snow in summer and rain in harvest,
So honor is not fitting for a fool.
texts.ESV:
Like snow in summer or rain in harvest,
    so honor is not fitting for a fool.
texts.NLT:
Honor is no more associated with fools
    than snow with summer or rain with harvest.
texts.LEB:
Like snow in the summer and like rain at the harvest,
    so honor is not fitting for a fool.

texts.NIV84: Like a fluttering sparrow or a darting swallow, an undeserved curse does not come to rest.
texts.KJV: As the bird by wandering, as the swallow by flying, so the curse causeless shall not come.
texts.NASB95:
Like a sparrow in its flitting, like a swallow in its flying,
So a curse without cause does not alight.
texts.DRB: As a bird flying to other places, and a sparrow going here or there: so a curse uttered without cause shall come upon a man.
texts.NKJV:
Like a flitting sparrow, like a flying swallow,
So a curse without cause shall not alight.
texts.ESV:
Like a sparrow in its flitting, like a swallow in its flying,
    a curse that is causeless does not alight.
texts.NLT:
Like a fluttering sparrow or a darting swallow,
    an undeserved curse will not land on its intended victim.
texts.LEB:
Like the sparrow is to fluttering and like the swallow is to flying,
    so an undeserved curse does not go forth.

texts.NIV84: A whip for the horse, a halter for the donkey, and a rod for the backs of fools!
texts.KJV: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the fool's back.
texts.NASB95:
A whip is for the horse, a bridle for the donkey,
And a rod for the back of fools.
texts.DRB: A whip for a horse, and a snaffle for an ass, and a rod for the back of fools.
texts.NKJV:
A whip for the horse,
A bridle for the donkey,
And a rod for the fool’s back.
texts.ESV:
A whip for the horse, a bridle for the donkey,
    and a rod for the back of fools.
texts.NLT:
Guide a horse with a whip, a donkey with a bridle,
    and a fool with a rod to his back!
texts.LEB:
A whip for the horse, a bridle for the donkey,
    and a rod for the back of fools.

texts.NIV84: Do not answer a fool according to his folly, or you will be like him yourself.
texts.KJV: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him.
texts.NASB95:
Do not answer a fool according to his folly,
Or you will also be like him.
texts.DRB: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou be made like him.
texts.NKJV:
Do not answer a fool according to his folly,
Lest you also be like him.
texts.ESV:
Answer not a fool according to his folly,
    lest you be like him yourself.
texts.NLT:
Don’t answer the foolish arguments of fools,
    or you will become as foolish as they are.
texts.LEB:
Do not answer a fool according to his folly
    lest you become like him—even you.

texts.NIV84: Answer a fool according to his folly, or he will be wise in his own eyes.
texts.KJV: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own conceit.
texts.NASB95:
Answer a fool as his folly deserves,
That he not be wise in his own eyes.
texts.DRB: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he imagine himself to be wise.
texts.NKJV:
Answer a fool according to his folly,
Lest he be wise in his own eyes.
texts.ESV:
Answer a fool according to his folly,
    lest he be wise in his own eyes.
texts.NLT:
Be sure to answer the foolish arguments of fools,
    or they will become wise in their own estimation.
texts.LEB:
Answer a fool according to his folly,
    or else he will be wise in his own eyes.

texts.NIV84: Like cutting off one's feet or drinking violence is the sending of a message by the hand of a fool.
texts.KJV: He that sendeth a message by the hand of a fool cutteth off the feet, and drinketh damage.
texts.NASB95:
He cuts off his own feet and drinks violence
Who sends a message by the hand of a fool.
texts.DRB: He that sendeth words by a foolish messenger, is lame of feet and drinketh iniquity.
texts.NKJV:
He who sends a message by the hand of a fool
Cuts off his own feet and drinks violence.
texts.ESV:
Whoever sends a message by the hand of a fool
    cuts off his own feet and drinks violence.
texts.NLT:
Trusting a fool to convey a message
    is like cutting off one’s feet or drinking poison!
texts.LEB:
Like cutting off feet or drinking violence,
    so is he who sends messages in the hand of a fool.

texts.NIV84: Like a lame man's legs that hang limp is a proverb in the mouth of a fool.
texts.KJV: The legs of the lame are not equal: so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
texts.NASB95:
Like the legs which are useless to the lame,
So is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
texts.DRB: As a lame man hath fair legs in vain: so a parable is unseemly in the mouth of fools.
texts.NKJV:
Like the legs of the lame that hang limp
Is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
texts.ESV:
Like a lame man's legs, which hang useless,
    is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
texts.NLT:
A proverb in the mouth of a fool
    is as useless as a paralyzed leg.
texts.LEB:
Like legs that hang limp from a lame person,
    so is a proverb in the mouth of fools.

texts.NIV84: Like tying a stone in a sling is the giving of honor to a fool.
texts.KJV: As he that bindeth a stone in a sling, so is he that giveth honour to a fool.
texts.NASB95:
Like one who binds a stone in a sling,
So is he who gives honor to a fool.
texts.DRB: As he that casteth a stone into the heap of Mercury: so is he that giveth honour to a fool.
texts.NKJV:
Like one who binds a stone in a sling
Is he who gives honor to a fool.
texts.ESV:
Like one who binds the stone in the sling
    is one who gives honor to a fool.
texts.NLT:
Honoring a fool
    is as foolish as tying a stone to a slingshot.
texts.LEB:
Like binding a stone in a sling,
    so is giving honor to a fool.

texts.NIV84: Like a thornbush in a drunkard's hand is a proverb in the mouth of a fool.
texts.KJV: As a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouths of fools.
texts.NASB95:
Like a thorn which falls into the hand of a drunkard,
So is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
texts.DRB: As if a thorn should grow in the hand of a drunkard: so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
texts.NKJV:
Like a thorn that goes into the hand of a drunkard
Is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
texts.ESV:
Like a thorn that goes up into the hand of a drunkard
    is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
texts.NLT:
A proverb in the mouth of a fool
    is like a thorny branch brandished by a drunk.
texts.LEB:
Like a thorn that goes up in the hand of a drunkard,
    so is a proverb in the mouth of fools.

texts.NIV84: Like an archer who wounds at random is he who hires a fool or any passer-by.
texts.KJV: The great God that formed all things both rewardeth the fool, and rewardeth transgressors.
texts.NASB95:
Like an archer who wounds everyone,
So is he who hires a fool or who hires those who pass by.
texts.DRB: Judgment determineth causes: and he that putteth a fool to silence, appeaseth anger.
texts.NKJV:
The great God who formed everything
Gives the fool his hire and the transgressor his wages.
texts.ESV:
Like an archer who wounds everyone
    is one who hires a passing fool or drunkard.
texts.NLT:
An employer who hires a fool or a bystander
    is like an archer who shoots at random.
texts.LEB:
Like an archer who wounds everyone,
    so is he who hires a fool or he who hires passersby.

texts.NIV84: As a dog returns to its vomit, so a fool repeats his folly.
texts.KJV: As a dog returneth to his vomit, so a fool returneth to his folly.
texts.NASB95:
Like a dog that returns to its vomit
Is a fool who repeats his folly.
texts.DRB: As a dog that returneth to his vomit, so is the fool that repeateth his folly.
texts.NKJV:
As a dog returns to his own vomit,
So a fool repeats his folly.
texts.ESV:
Like a dog that returns to his vomit
    is a fool who repeats his folly.
texts.NLT:
As a dog returns to its vomit,
    so a fool repeats his foolishness.
texts.LEB:
Like a dog returning to his vomit
    is a fool reverting to his folly.

texts.NIV84: Do you see a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
texts.KJV: Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
texts.NASB95:
Do you see a man wise in his own eyes?
There is more hope for a fool than for him.
texts.DRB: Hast thou seen a man wise in his own conceit? there shall be more hope of a fool than of him.
texts.NKJV:
Do you see a man wise in his own eyes?
There is more hope for a fool than for him.
texts.ESV:
Do you see a man who is wise in his own eyes?
    There is more hope for a fool than for him.
texts.NLT:
There is more hope for fools
    than for people who think they are wise.
texts.LEB:
Do you see a man wise in his own eyes?
    There is more hope for a fool than for him.

texts.NIV84: The sluggard says, "There is a lion in the road, a fierce lion roaming the streets!"
texts.KJV: The slothful man saith, There is a lion in the way; a lion is in the streets.
texts.NASB95:
The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the road!
A lion is in the open square!”
texts.DRB: The slothful man saith: There is a lion in the way, and a lioness in the roads.
texts.NKJV:
The lazy man says, “There is a lion in the road!
A fierce lion is in the streets!”
texts.ESV:
The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the road!
    There is a lion in the streets!”
texts.NLT:
The lazy person claims, “There’s a lion on the road!
    Yes, I’m sure there’s a lion out there!”
texts.LEB:
A lazy person says “A lion is in the road!
    A lion among the streets!”

texts.NIV84: As a door turns on its hinges, so a sluggard turns on his bed.
texts.KJV: As the door turneth upon his hinges, so doth the slothful upon his bed.
texts.NASB95:
As the door turns on its hinges,
So does the sluggard on his bed.
texts.DRB: As the door turneth upon its hinges, so doth the slothful upon his bed.
texts.NKJV:
As a door turns on its hinges,
So does the lazy man on his bed.
texts.ESV:
As a door turns on its hinges,
    so does a sluggard on his bed.
texts.NLT:
As a door swings back and forth on its hinges,
    so the lazy person turns over in bed.
texts.LEB:
The door turns on its hinge,
    and a lazy person on his bed.

texts.NIV84: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish; he is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.
texts.KJV: The slothful hideth his hand in his bosom; it grieveth him to bring it again to his mouth.
texts.NASB95:
The sluggard buries his hand in the dish;
He is weary of bringing it to his mouth again.
texts.DRB: The slothful hideth his hand under his armpit, and it grieveth him to turn it to his mouth.
texts.NKJV:
The lazy man buries his hand in the bowl;
It wearies him to bring it back to his mouth.
texts.ESV:
The sluggard buries his hand in the dish;
    it wears him out to bring it back to his mouth.
texts.NLT:
Lazy people take food in their hand
    but don’t even lift it to their mouth.
texts.LEB:
A lazy person buries his hands in the dish;
    he is too tired to return it to his mouth.

texts.NIV84: The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who answer discreetly.
texts.KJV: The sluggard is wiser in his own conceit than seven men that can render a reason.
texts.NASB95:
The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes
Than seven men who can give a discreet answer.
texts.DRB: The sluggard is wiser in his own conceit, than seven men that speak sentences.
texts.NKJV:
The lazy man is wiser in his own eyes
Than seven men who can answer sensibly.
texts.ESV:
The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes
    than seven men who can answer sensibly.
texts.NLT:
Lazy people consider themselves smarter
    than seven wise counselors.
texts.LEB:
A lazy person is wiser in his eyes
    than seven who answer discreetly.

texts.NIV84: Like one who seizes a dog by the ears is a passer-by who meddles in a quarrel not his own.
texts.KJV: He that passeth by, and meddleth with strife belonging not to him, is like one that taketh a dog by the ears.
texts.NASB95:
Like one who takes a dog by the ears
Is he who passes by and meddles with strife not belonging to him.
texts.DRB: As he that taketh a dog by the ears, so is he that passeth by in anger, and meddleth with another man's quarrel.
texts.NKJV:
He who passes by and meddles in a quarrel not his own
Is like one who takes a dog by the ears.
texts.ESV:
Whoever meddles in a quarrel not his own
    is like one who takes a passing dog by the ears.
texts.NLT:
Interfering in someone else’s argument
    is as foolish as yanking a dog’s ears.
texts.LEB:
Grabbing onto the ears of a dog
    passing by is one who meddles in a quarrel that is not his own.

texts.NIV84: Like a madman shooting firebrands or deadly arrows
texts.KJV: As a mad man who casteth firebrands, arrows, and death,
texts.NASB95:
Like a madman who throws
Firebrands, arrows and death,
texts.DRB: As he is guilty that shooteth arrows, and lances unto death :
texts.NKJV: Like a madman who throws firebrands, arrows, and death,
texts.ESV: Like a madman who throws firebrands, arrows, and death
texts.NLT:
Just as damaging
    as a madman shooting a deadly weapon
texts.LEB:
Like a maniac who shoots
    firebrands, arrows, and death,

texts.NIV84: is a man who deceives his neighbor and says, "I was only joking!"
texts.KJV: So is the man that deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Am not I in sport?
texts.NASB95:
So is the man who deceives his neighbor,
And says, “Was I not joking?”
texts.DRB: So is the man that hurteth his friend deceitfully: and when he is taken, saith: I did it in jest.
texts.NKJV:
Is the man who deceives his neighbor,
And says, “I was only joking!”
texts.ESV:
is the man who deceives his neighbor
    and says, “I am only joking!”
texts.NLT:
is someone who lies to a friend
    and then says, “I was only joking.”
texts.LEB:
so is a man who deceives his neighbor,
    but says “Am I not joking?”

texts.NIV84: Without wood a fire goes out; without gossip a quarrel dies down.
texts.KJV: Where no wood is, there the fire goeth out: so where there is no talebearer, the strife ceaseth.
texts.NASB95:
For lack of wood the fire goes out,
And where there is no whisperer, contention quiets down.
texts.DRB: When the wood faileth, the fire shall go out: and when the talebearer is taken away, contentions shall cease.
texts.NKJV:
Where there is no wood, the fire goes out;
And where there is no talebearer, strife ceases.
texts.ESV:
For lack of wood the fire goes out,
    and where there is no whisperer, quarreling ceases.
texts.NLT:
Fire goes out without wood,
    and quarrels disappear when gossip stops.
texts.LEB:
For lack of wood, a fire goes out,
    and where there is no whisperer, quarreling will cease.

texts.NIV84: As charcoal to embers and as wood to fire, so is a quarrelsome man for kindling strife.
texts.KJV: As coals are to burning coals, and wood to fire; so is a contentious man to kindle strife.
texts.NASB95:
Like charcoal to hot embers and wood to fire,
So is a contentious man to kindle strife.
texts.DRB: As coals are to burning coals, and wood to fire, so an angry man stirreth up strife.
texts.NKJV:
As charcoal is to burning coals, and wood to fire,
So is a contentious man to kindle strife.
texts.ESV:
As charcoal to hot embers and wood to fire,
    so is a quarrelsome man for kindling strife.
texts.NLT:
A quarrelsome person starts fights
    as easily as hot embers light charcoal or fire lights wood.
texts.LEB:
As charcoal is to hot embers and wood is to fire,
    so a man of quarrels is to kindling strife.

texts.NIV84: The words of a gossip are like choice morsels; they go down to a man's inmost parts.
texts.KJV: The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.
texts.NASB95:
The words of a whisperer are like dainty morsels,
And they go down into the innermost parts of the body.
texts.DRB: The words of a talebearer are as it were simple, but they reach to the innermost parts of the belly.
texts.NKJV:
The words of a talebearer are like tasty trifles,
And they go down into the inmost body.
texts.ESV:
The words of a whisperer are like delicious morsels;
    they go down into the inner parts of the body.
texts.NLT:
Rumors are dainty morsels
    that sink deep into one’s heart.
texts.LEB:
The words of a whisperer are like delicious morsels,
    and they go down to the inner parts of the body.

texts.NIV84: Like a coating of glaze over earthenware are fervent lips with an evil heart.
texts.KJV: Burning lips and a wicked heart are like a potsherd covered with silver dross.
texts.NASB95:
Like an earthen vessel overlaid with silver dross
Are burning lips and a wicked heart.
texts.DRB: Swelling lips joined with a corrupt heart, are like an earthen vessel adorned with silver dross.
texts.NKJV:
Fervent lips with a wicked heart
Are like earthenware covered with silver dross.
texts.ESV:
Like the glaze covering an earthen vessel
    are fervent lips with an evil heart.
texts.NLT:
Smooth words may hide a wicked heart,
    just as a pretty glaze covers a clay pot.
texts.LEB:
Like impure silver which overlays an earthen vessel,
    so are smooth lips and an evil heart.

texts.NIV84: A malicious man disguises himself with his lips, but in his heart he harbors deceit.
texts.KJV: He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, and layeth up deceit within him;
texts.NASB95:
He who hates disguises it with his lips,
But he lays up deceit in his heart.
texts.DRB: An enemy is known by his lips, when in his heart he entertaineth deceit.
texts.NKJV:
He who hates, disguises it with his lips,
And lays up deceit within himself;
texts.ESV:
Whoever hates disguises himself with his lips
    and harbors deceit in his heart;
texts.NLT:
People may cover their hatred with pleasant words,
    but they’re deceiving you.
texts.LEB:
On his lips, an enemy will pretend,
    but inside he will harbor deceit.

texts.NIV84: Though his speech is charming, do not believe him, for seven abominations fill his heart.
texts.KJV: When he speaketh fair, believe him not: for there are seven abominations in his heart.
texts.NASB95:
When he speaks graciously, do not believe him,
For there are seven abominations in his heart.
texts.DRB: When he shall speak low, trust him not: because there are seven mischiefs in his heart.
texts.NKJV:
When he speaks kindly, do not believe him,
For there are seven abominations in his heart;
texts.ESV:
when he speaks graciously, believe him not,
    for there are seven abominations in his heart;
texts.NLT:
They pretend to be kind, but don’t believe them.
    Their hearts are full of many evils.
texts.LEB:
When he makes his voice gracious, do not believe him,
    for seven abominations are in his heart.

texts.NIV84: His malice may be concealed by deception, but his wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.
texts.KJV: Whose hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness shall be shewed before the whole congregation.
texts.NASB95:
Though his hatred covers itself with guile,
His wickedness will be revealed before the assembly.
texts.DRB: He that covereth hatred deceitfully, his malice shall be laid open in the public assembly.
texts.NKJV:
Though his hatred is covered by deceit,
His wickedness will be revealed before the assembly.
texts.ESV:
though his hatred be covered with deception,
    his wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.
texts.NLT:
While their hatred may be concealed by trickery,
    their wrongdoing will be exposed in public.
texts.LEB:
Though hatred is covered with guile,
    its evil will be exposed in the assembly.

texts.NIV84: If a man digs a pit, he will fall into it; if a man rolls a stone, it will roll back on him.
texts.KJV: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will return upon him.
texts.NASB95:
He who digs a pit will fall into it,
And he who rolls a stone, it will come back on him.
texts.DRB: He that diggeth a pit, shall fall into it: and he that rolleth a stone, it shall return to him.
texts.NKJV:
Whoever digs a pit will fall into it,
And he who rolls a stone will have it roll back on him.
texts.ESV:
Whoever digs a pit will fall into it,
    and a stone will come back on him who starts it rolling.
texts.NLT:
If you set a trap for others,
    you will get caught in it yourself.
If you roll a boulder down on others,
    it will crush you instead.
texts.LEB:
He who digs a pit, in it he will fall,
    and he who rolls a stone, on him it will come back.

texts.NIV84: A lying tongue hates those it hurts, and a flattering mouth works ruin.
texts.KJV: A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.
texts.NASB95:
A lying tongue hates those it crushes,
And a flattering mouth works ruin.
texts.DRB: A deceitful tongue loveth not truth: and a slippery mouth worketh ruin.
texts.NKJV:
A lying tongue hates those who are crushed by it,
And a flattering mouth works ruin.
texts.ESV:
A lying tongue hates its victims,
    and a flattering mouth works ruin.
texts.NLT:
A lying tongue hates its victims,
    and flattering words cause ruin.
texts.LEB:
A tongue of deceit hates its victim,
    and a flattering mouth makes ruin.

blocks.title: A Mocker A Fool and A Slacker

texts.NIV84: Wine is a mocker and beer a brawler; whoever is led astray by them is not wise.
texts.KJV: Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise.
texts.NASB95:
Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler,
And whoever is intoxicated by it is not wise.
texts.DRB: Wine is a luxurious thing, and drunkenness riotous: whosoever is delighted therewith shell not be wise.
texts.NKJV:
Wine is a mocker,
Strong drink is a brawler,
And whoever is led astray by it is not wise.
texts.ESV:
Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler,
    and whoever is led astray by it is not wise.
texts.NLT:
Wine produces mockers; alcohol leads to brawls.
    Those led astray by drink cannot be wise.
texts.LEB:
Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler,
    and any who go astray by it are not wise.

texts.NIV84: A king's wrath is like the roar of a lion; he who angers him forfeits his life.
texts.KJV: The fear of a king is as the roaring of a lion: whoso provoketh him to anger sinneth against his own soul.
texts.NASB95:
The terror of a king is like the growling of a lion;
He who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.
texts.DRB: As the roaring of a lion, so also is the dread of a king: he that provoketh him, sinneth against his own soul.
texts.NKJV:
The wrath of a king is like the roaring of a lion;
Whoever provokes him to anger sins against his own life.
texts.ESV:
The terror of a king is like the growling of a lion;
    whoever provokes him to anger forfeits his life.
texts.NLT:
The king’s fury is like a lion’s roar;
    to rouse his anger is to risk your life.
texts.LEB:
Roaring like the lion is the dreaded anger of a king;
    he who provokes him forfeits his life.

texts.NIV84: It is to a man's honor to avoid strife, but every fool is quick to quarrel.
texts.KJV: It is an honour for a man to cease from strife: but every fool will be meddling.
texts.NASB95:
Keeping away from strife is an honor for a man,
But any fool will quarrel.
texts.DRB: It is an honour for a man to separate himself from quarrels: but all fools are meddling with reproaches.
texts.NKJV:
It is honorable for a man to stop striving,
Since any fool can start a quarrel.
texts.ESV:
It is an honor for a man to keep aloof from strife,
    but every fool will be quarreling.
texts.NLT:
Avoiding a fight is a mark of honor;
    only fools insist on quarreling.
texts.LEB:
It is honorable for the man to refrain from strife,
    but every fool will be quick to quarrel.

texts.NIV84: A sluggard does not plow in season; so at harvest time he looks but finds nothing.
texts.KJV: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have nothing.
texts.NASB95:
The sluggard does not plow after the autumn,
So he begs during the harvest and has nothing.
texts.DRB: Because of the cold the sluggard would not plough: he shall beg therefore in the summer, and it shall not be given him.
texts.NKJV:
The lazy man will not plow because of winter;
He will beg during harvest and have nothing.
texts.ESV:
The sluggard does not plow in the autumn;
    he will seek at harvest and have nothing.
texts.NLT:
Those too lazy to plow in the right season
    will have no food at the harvest.
texts.LEB:
The lazy person will not plow in season;
    he will expect at the harvest, but there will be nothing.

texts.NIV84: The purposes of a man's heart are deep waters, but a man of understanding draws them out.
texts.KJV: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.
texts.NASB95:
A plan in the heart of a man is like deep water,
But a man of understanding draws it out.
texts.DRB: Counsel in the heart of a man is like deep water: but a wise man will draw it out.
texts.NKJV:
Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water,
But a man of understanding will draw it out.
texts.ESV:
The purpose in a man's heart is like deep water,
    but a man of understanding will draw it out.
texts.NLT:
Though good advice lies deep within the heart,
    a person with understanding will draw it out.
texts.LEB:
Deep waters are like purpose in the heart of a man,
    and a man of understanding will draw it out.

texts.NIV84: Many a man claims to have unfailing love, but a faithful man who can find?
texts.KJV: Most men will proclaim every one his own goodness: but a faithful man who can find?
texts.NASB95:
Many a man proclaims his own loyalty,
But who can find a trustworthy man?
texts.DRB: Many men are called merciful: but who shall find a faithful man?
texts.NKJV:
Most men will proclaim each his own goodness,
But who can find a faithful man?
texts.ESV:
Many a man proclaims his own steadfast love,
    but a faithful man who can find?
texts.NLT:
Many will say they are loyal friends,
    but who can find one who is truly reliable?
texts.LEB:
Many a person will proclaim his loyalty for himself,
    but a man who is trustworthy, who can find?

texts.NIV84: The righteous man leads a blameless life; blessed are his children after him.
texts.KJV: The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him.
texts.NASB95:
A righteous man who walks in his integrity—
How blessed are his sons after him.
texts.DRB: The just that walketh in his simplicity, shall leave behind him blessed children.
texts.NKJV:
The righteous man walks in his integrity;
His children are blessed after him.
texts.ESV:
The righteous who walks in his integrity—
    blessed are his children after him!
texts.NLT:
The godly walk with integrity;
    blessed are their children who follow them.
texts.LEB:
He who walks in his integrity is righteous;
    happy are his children who follow him.

texts.NIV84: When a king sits on his throne to judge, he winnows out all evil with his eyes.
texts.KJV: A king that sitteth in the throne of judgment scattereth away all evil with his eyes.
texts.NASB95:
A king who sits on the throne of justice
Disperses all evil with his eyes.
texts.DRB: The king, that sitteth on the throne of judgment, scattereth away all evil with his look.
texts.NKJV:
A king who sits on the throne of judgment
Scatters all evil with his eyes.
texts.ESV:
A king who sits on the throne of judgment
    winnows all evil with his eyes.
texts.NLT:
When a king sits in judgment, he weighs all the evidence,
    distinguishing the bad from the good.
texts.LEB:
A king who sits on the throne of judgment
    winnows all evil with his eyes.

texts.NIV84: Who can say, "I have kept my heart pure; I am clean and without sin"?
texts.KJV: Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?
texts.NASB95:
Who can say, “I have cleansed my heart,
I am pure from my sin”?
texts.DRB: Who can say: My heart is clean, I am pure from sin?
texts.NKJV:
Who can say, “I have made my heart clean,
I am pure from my sin”?
texts.ESV:
Who can say, “I have made my heart pure;
    I am clean from my sin”?
texts.NLT:
Who can say, “I have cleansed my heart;
    I am pure and free from sin”?
texts.LEB:
Who will say “I have made my heart clean;
    I am pure from my sin”?

texts.NIV84: Differing weights and differing measures- the LORD detests them both.
texts.KJV: Divers weights, and divers measures, both of them are alike abomination to the Lord.
texts.NASB95:
Differing weights and differing measures,
Both of them are abominable to the Lord.
texts.DRB: Diverse weights and diverse measures, both are abominable before God.
texts.NKJV:
Diverse weights and diverse measures,
They are both alike, an abomination to the Lord.
texts.ESV:
Unequal weights and unequal measures
    are both alike an abomination to the Lord.
texts.NLT:
False weights and unequal measures—
    the Lord detests double standards of every kind.
texts.LEB:
Stone and stone, measure and measure,
    both of them are an abomination of Yahweh.

texts.NIV84: Even a child is known by his actions, by whether his conduct is pure and right.
texts.KJV: Even a child is known by his doings, whether his work be pure, and whether it be right.
texts.NASB95:
It is by his deeds that a lad distinguishes himself
If his conduct is pure and right.
texts.DRB: By his inclinations a child is known, if his works be clean and right.
texts.NKJV:
Even a child is known by his deeds,
Whether what he does is pure and right.
texts.ESV:
Even a child makes himself known by his acts,
    by whether his conduct is pure and upright.
texts.NLT:
Even children are known by the way they act,
    whether their conduct is pure, and whether it is right.
texts.LEB:
Even by his acts, a young man will make himself known,
    whether his acts are pure and upright.

texts.NIV84: Ears that hear and eyes that see- the LORD has made them both.
texts.KJV: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the Lord hath made even both of them.
texts.NASB95:
The hearing ear and the seeing eye,
The Lord has made both of them.
texts.DRB: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the Lord hath made them both.
texts.NKJV:
The hearing ear and the seeing eye,
The Lord has made them both.
texts.ESV:
The hearing ear and the seeing eye,
    the Lord has made them both.
texts.NLT:
Ears to hear and eyes to see—
    both are gifts from the Lord.
texts.LEB:
The ear that hears and the eye that sees,
    Yahweh has made them both.

texts.NIV84: Do not love sleep or you will grow poor; stay awake and you will have food to spare.
texts.KJV: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, and thou shalt be satisfied with bread.
texts.NASB95:
Do not love sleep, or you will become poor;
Open your eyes, and you will be satisfied with food.
texts.DRB: Love not sleep, lest poverty oppress thee: open thy eyes, and be filled with bread.
texts.NKJV:
Do not love sleep, lest you come to poverty;
Open your eyes, and you will be satisfied with bread.
texts.ESV:
Love not sleep, lest you come to poverty;
    open your eyes, and you will have plenty of bread.
texts.NLT:
If you love sleep, you will end in poverty.
    Keep your eyes open, and there will be plenty to eat!
texts.LEB:
Do not love sleep, lest you become poor;
    open your eyes and have plenty bread.

texts.NIV84: "It's no good, it's no good!" says the buyer; then off he goes and boasts about his purchase.
texts.KJV: It is naught, it is naught, saith the buyer: but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.
texts.NASB95:
“Bad, bad,” says the buyer,
But when he goes his way, then he boasts.
texts.DRB: It is nought, it is nought, saith every buyer: and when he is gone away, then he will boast.
texts.NKJV:
“It is good for nothing,” cries the buyer;
But when he has gone his way, then he boasts.
texts.ESV:
“Bad, bad,” says the buyer,
    but when he goes away, then he boasts.
texts.NLT:
The buyer haggles over the price, saying, “It’s worthless,”
    then brags about getting a bargain!
texts.LEB:
“Bad, bad,” the buyer will say,
    but when one goes to him, then he will boast.

texts.NIV84: Gold there is, and rubies in abundance, but lips that speak knowledge are a rare jewel.
texts.KJV: There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
texts.NASB95:
There is gold, and an abundance of jewels;
But the lips of knowledge are a more precious thing.
texts.DRB: There is gold, and a multitude of jewels: but the lips of knowledge are a precious vessel.
texts.NKJV:
There is gold and a multitude of rubies,
But the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
texts.ESV:
There is gold and abundance of costly stones,
    but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
texts.NLT:
Wise words are more valuable
    than much gold and many rubies.
texts.LEB:
There is gold and many costly stones,
    but precious jewels are lips of knowledge.

texts.NIV84: Take the garment of one who puts up security for a stranger; hold it in pledge if he does it for a wayward woman.
texts.KJV: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger: and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.
texts.NASB95:
Take his garment when he becomes surety for a stranger;
And for foreigners, hold him in pledge.
texts.DRB: Take away the garment of him that is surety for a stranger, and take a pledge from him for strangers.
texts.NKJV:
Take the garment of one who is surety for a stranger,
And hold it as a pledge when it is for a seductress.
texts.ESV:
Take a man's garment when he has put up security for a stranger,
    and hold it in pledge when he puts up security for foreigners.
texts.NLT:
Get security from someone who guarantees a stranger’s debt.
    Get a deposit if he does it for foreigners.
texts.LEB:
Take his garment, for he has given security to a stranger,
    and on behalf of a foreigner—take it as pledge.

texts.NIV84: Food gained by fraud tastes sweet to a man, but he ends up with a mouth full of gravel.
texts.KJV: Bread of deceit is sweet to a man; but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.
texts.NASB95:
Bread obtained by falsehood is sweet to a man,
But afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
texts.DRB: The bread of lying is sweet to a man: but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.
texts.NKJV:
Bread gained by deceit is sweet to a man,
But afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
texts.ESV:
Bread gained by deceit is sweet to a man,
    but afterward his mouth will be full of gravel.
texts.NLT:
Stolen bread tastes sweet,
    but it turns to gravel in the mouth.
texts.LEB:
Bread gained by deceit is sweet for the man,
    but afterward, his mouth will be filled with gravel.

texts.NIV84: Make plans by seeking advice; if you wage war, obtain guidance.
texts.KJV: Every purpose is established by counsel: and with good advice make war.
texts.NASB95:
Prepare plans by consultation,
And make war by wise guidance.
texts.DRB: Designs are strengthened by counsels: and wars are to be managed by governments.
texts.NKJV:
Plans are established by counsel;
By wise counsel wage war.
texts.ESV:
Plans are established by counsel;
    by wise guidance wage war.
texts.NLT:
Plans succeed through good counsel;
    don’t go to war without wise advice.
texts.LEB:
A plan will be established by advice,
    and with guidance make war.

texts.NIV84: A gossip betrays a confidence; so avoid a man who talks too much.
texts.KJV: He that goeth about as a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips.
texts.NASB95:
He who goes about as a slanderer reveals secrets,
Therefore do not associate with a gossip.
texts.DRB: Meddle not with him that revealeth secrets, and walketh deceitfully, and openeth wide his lips.
texts.NKJV:
He who goes about as a talebearer reveals secrets;
Therefore do not associate with one who flatters with his lips.
texts.ESV:
Whoever goes about slandering reveals secrets;
    therefore do not associate with a simple babbler.
texts.NLT:
A gossip goes around telling secrets,
    so don’t hang around with chatterers.
texts.LEB:
He reveals a secret, he who walks about with gossip,
    and do not associate with a babbler’s lips.

texts.NIV84: If a man curses his father or mother, his lamp will be snuffed out in pitch darkness.
texts.KJV: Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness.
texts.NASB95:
He who curses his father or his mother,
His lamp will go out in time of darkness.
texts.DRB: He that curseth his father, and mother, his lamp shall be put out in the midst of darkness.
texts.NKJV:
Whoever curses his father or his mother,
His lamp will be put out in deep darkness.
texts.ESV:
If one curses his father or his mother,
    his lamp will be put out in utter darkness.
texts.NLT:
If you insult your father or mother,
    your light will be snuffed out in total darkness.
texts.LEB:
He who curses his father and his mother,
    his lamp will be extinguished in the midst of darkness.

texts.NIV84: An inheritance quickly gained at the beginning will not be blessed at the end.
texts.KJV: An inheritance may be gotten hastily at the beginning; but the end thereof shall not be blessed.
texts.NASB95:
An inheritance gained hurriedly at the beginning
Will not be blessed in the end.
texts.DRB: The inheritance gotten hastily in the beginning, in the end shall be without a blessing.
texts.NKJV:
An inheritance gained hastily at the beginning
Will not be blessed at the end.
texts.ESV:
An inheritance gained hastily in the beginning
    will not be blessed in the end.
texts.NLT:
An inheritance obtained too early in life
    is not a blessing in the end.
texts.LEB:
An inheritance acquired hastily at the beginning
    will not be blessed at its end.

texts.NIV84: Do not say, "I'll pay you back for this wrong!" Wait for the LORD, and he will deliver you.
texts.KJV: Say not thou, I will recompense evil; but wait on the Lord, and he shall save thee.
texts.NASB95:
Do not say, “I will repay evil”;
Wait for the Lord, and He will save you.
texts.DRB: Say not: I will return evil: wait for the Lord and he will deliver thee.
texts.NKJV:
Do not say, “I will recompense evil”;
Wait for the Lord, and He will save you.
texts.ESV:
Do not say, “I will repay evil”;
    wait for the Lord, and he will deliver you.
texts.NLT:
Don’t say, “I will get even for this wrong.”
    Wait for the Lord to handle the matter.
texts.LEB:
Do not say “I will repay evil”;
    wait for Yahweh and he will deliver you.

texts.NIV84: The LORD detests differing weights, and dishonest scales do not please him.
texts.KJV: Divers weights are an abomination unto the Lord; and a false balance is not good.
texts.NASB95:
Differing weights are an abomination to the Lord,
And a false scale is not good.
texts.DRB: Diverse weights are an abomination before the Lord: a deceitful balance is not good.
texts.NKJV:
Diverse weights are an abomination to the Lord,
And dishonest scales are not good.
texts.ESV:
Unequal weights are an abomination to the Lord,
    and false scales are not good.
texts.NLT:
The Lord detests double standards;
    he is not pleased by dishonest scales.
texts.LEB:
An abomination of Yahweh is a stone and a weight,
    and scales of falsehood are not good.

texts.NIV84: A man's steps are directed by the LORD. How then can anyone understand his own way?
texts.KJV: Man's goings are of the Lord; how can a man then understand his own way?
texts.NASB95:
Man’s steps are ordained by the Lord,
How then can man understand his way?
texts.DRB: The steps of man are guided by the Lord: but who is the man that can understand his own way?
texts.NKJV:
A man’s steps are of the Lord;
How then can a man understand his own way?
texts.ESV:
A man's steps are from the Lord;
    how then can man understand his way?
texts.NLT:
The Lord directs our steps,
    so why try to understand everything along the way?
texts.LEB:
Away from Yahweh are the steps of a strong man,
    and how will humankind understand his ways?

texts.NIV84: It is a trap for a man to dedicate something rashly and only later to consider his vows.
texts.KJV: It is a snare to the man who devoureth that which is holy, and after vows to make enquiry.
texts.NASB95:
It is a trap for a man to say rashly, “It is holy!”
And after the vows to make inquiry.
texts.DRB: It is ruin to a man to devour holy ones, and after vows to retract.
texts.NKJV:
It is a snare for a man to devote rashly something as holy,
And afterward to reconsider his vows.
texts.ESV:
It is a snare to say rashly, “It is holy,”
    and to reflect only after making vows.
texts.NLT:
Don’t trap yourself by making a rash promise to God
    and only later counting the cost.
texts.LEB:
It is a snare to humankind to say rashly “It is holy,”
    and after vows, to scrutinize.

texts.NIV84: A wise king winnows out the wicked; he drives the threshing wheel over them.
texts.KJV: A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.
texts.NASB95:
A wise king winnows the wicked,
And drives the threshing wheel over them.
texts.DRB: A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth over them the wheel.
texts.NKJV:
A wise king sifts out the wicked,
And brings the threshing wheel over them.
texts.ESV:
A wise king winnows the wicked
    and drives the wheel over them.
texts.NLT:
A wise king scatters the wicked like wheat,
    then runs his threshing wheel over them.
texts.LEB:
A wise king winnows the wicked,
    and he will drive a wheel over them.

texts.NIV84: The lamp of the LORD searches the spirit of a man; it searches out his inmost being.
texts.KJV: The spirit of man is the candle of the Lord, searching all the inward parts of the belly.
texts.NASB95:
The spirit of man is the lamp of the Lord,
Searching all the innermost parts of his being.
texts.DRB: The spirit of a man is the lamp of the Lord, which searcheth all the hidden things of the bowels.
texts.NKJV:
The spirit of a man is the lamp of the Lord,
Searching all the inner depths of his heart.
texts.ESV:
The spirit of man is the lamp of the Lord,
    searching all his innermost parts.
texts.NLT:
The Lord’s light penetrates the human spirit,
    exposing every hidden motive.
texts.LEB:
The lamp of Yahweh is the spirit of humankind,
    he who searches every innermost part.

texts.NIV84: Love and faithfulness keep a king safe; through love his throne is made secure.
texts.KJV: Mercy and truth preserve the king: and his throne is upholden by mercy.
texts.NASB95:
Loyalty and truth preserve the king,
And he upholds his throne by righteousness.
texts.DRB: Mercy and truth preserve the king, and his throne is strengthened by clemency.
texts.NKJV:
Mercy and truth preserve the king,
And by lovingkindness he upholds his throne.
texts.ESV:
Steadfast love and faithfulness preserve the king,
    and by steadfast love his throne is upheld.
texts.NLT:
Unfailing love and faithfulness protect the king;
    his throne is made secure through love.
texts.LEB:
Loyalty and faithfulness will preserve a king,
    and he is upheld with the righteousness of his throne.

texts.NIV84: The glory of young men is their strength, gray hair the splendor of the old.
texts.KJV: The glory of young men is their strength: and the beauty of old men is the grey head.
texts.NASB95:
The glory of young men is their strength,
And the honor of old men is their gray hair.
texts.DRB: The joy of young men is their strength: and the dignity of old men, their grey hairs.
texts.NKJV:
The glory of young men is their strength,
And the splendor of old men is their gray head.
texts.ESV:
The glory of young men is their strength,
    but the splendor of old men is their gray hair.
texts.NLT:
The glory of the young is their strength;
    the gray hair of experience is the splendor of the old.
texts.LEB:
The glory of young men is their strength,
    but the beauty of the aged is gray hair.

texts.NIV84: Blows and wounds cleanse away evil, and beatings purge the inmost being.
texts.KJV: The blueness of a wound cleanseth away evil: so do stripes the inward parts of the belly.
texts.NASB95:
Stripes that wound scour away evil,
And strokes reach the innermost parts.
texts.DRB: The blueness of a wound shall wipe away evils: and stripes in the more in- ward parts of the belly.
texts.NKJV:
Blows that hurt cleanse away evil,
As do stripes the inner depths of the heart.
texts.ESV:
Blows that wound cleanse away evil;
    strokes make clean the innermost parts.
texts.NLT:
Physical punishment cleanses away evil;
    such discipline purifies the heart.
texts.LEB:
The blows of a wound will cleanse evil,
    as will beatings of the innermost part.
